title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot reveals that Samuel Kidder used a deceptive, unkempt appearance—including a half-shaved beard and stained clothing—to conceal facial scratches he sustained while climbing down from Richard Nigges's room window. Poirot clarifies that while Kidder assisted the murderer, he was not the one who killed Nigges.

Poirot identifies the three individuals present in Room 317 at 7:15 PM, the time Rafael Bacard delivered afternoon tea. The occupants were the deceased Edith Gransbury, who had been posed on a chair to appear alive; Samuel Kidder, who was impersonating Richard Nigges; and the portrait painter Nancy Drew. Nancy admits she had disguised herself as Harriet Sipper to gossip and avoid being recognized by Raffles Burbach. She further reveals that Sir John and Lady Wallace provided her with a false alibi, claiming she was not at the Blauxham Hotel on the night of the incident.

Poirot explains how he deduced the connection between the parties. He recognized a unique coffee served by a "clumsy" new maid, Dulcinites, as being from "The Happy Coffee House." He linked this to Jane Hobbs, who had previously worked for Lady Wallace and frequently shopped at that establishment. Poirot concludes that Lady Wallace attempted to deceive the investigation by presenting a portrait by Nancy Drew as a recent gift. He notes that the painting lacked Nancy's customary date and initials, "NAED," because Lady Wallace had erased them to make the work appear newly completed to mislead the police.
